what_it_does: Runs a deterministic sequence planner for the pitch arc, expands role run lengths to 16 pages from sequence rules, assigns archetypes and dial values, then performs proof checks against expected deck structure and referenced records.
observations: Reads sequence, layout, and token JSON records relative to the engine directory.
Expands each run from its minimum count toward its maximum count in listed order until the requested page count is reached.
Normalizes archetype affinity labels by removing parenthetical suffixes before resolution and validation.
For each page, selects an archetype from the role affinity list, cycles within a run, and forces a different archetype at role boundaries when possible.
Assigns dial values from the role envelope using midpoint warmth, first register, and density as stored.
Contains an executable proof for plan("pitch", 16) with process exit status tied to pass/fail results.
standouts: The planner and proof live in the same executable script.
The proof checks both generated structure and referential integrity against layout archetypes and token registers.
Run expansion is deterministic and order-sensitive.
conventions: Uses ESM imports and Node built-ins for filesystem/path access.
Keeps proof output as console text with PASS/FAIL lines and a final summary.
Uses compact inline helpers and top-level script execution.
Represents expected deck structure as role/count tuples.
concerns: plan assumes the arc name, role names, count arrays, affinity arrays, and dial envelope fields exist without local guards.
The boundary archetype change only tries aff[1], so a repeated boundary can still persist if aff[1] matches the prior archetype or the affinity list has one item.
The proof logic is fixed to pitch at 16 pages rather than parameterized.
notes: A self-checking resolver that turns narrative-role run rules into a concrete 16-page pitch deck plan and verifies the generated shape.
questions: Should plan be reusable as an imported function, or is this file intended only as a proof executable?
Are single-affinity roles allowed to repeat archetypes across boundaries by design?
Should register selection always use the first envelope entry?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it resolves a sequence plan and proves that the pitch arc at 16 pages reproduces the expected deck structure and validates referenced records.
intention_signals: It seems intended to demonstrate that a deck page sequence can be re-derived from compact narrative rules rather than manually enumerated page records.
novelty: Combines a deterministic layout-sequence resolver with a file-local proof that validates narrative shape, archetype transitions, warmth choreography, and cross-record references.
contribution: Contributes a deterministic bridge from narrative arc rules to concrete page plans, plus local proof checks that a generated pitch sequence conforms to expected structural and reference constraints.
summary_for_embedding: Executable Node proof script that expands sequence arc run rules into a deterministic 16-page pitch deck plan and validates structure, archetypes, warmth, and register references.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to show that the pitch deck sequence can be generated from recorded rules and checked against expected deck-level behavior.

symbols.does: Removes a trailing parenthetical qualifier from an archetype affinity label.
symbols.description: This helper normalizes labels such as "split-bleed (timeline)" to "split-bleed" before archetype selection and validation. It treats the parenthetical portion as descriptive decoration rather than part of the archetype id.

symbols.does: Generates a page plan for an arc at a requested page count using run rules, role affinities, and dial envelopes.
symbols.description: The function starts each arc run at its minimum length, grows runs toward their maximums in listed order until the requested total is reached, and throws if the total cannot be met. It emits page objects with role, archetype, and dials, cycling archetypes within runs and attempting to change archetype at each role boundary.

symbols.does: Prints a named PASS or FAIL result and increments the corresponding proof counter.
symbols.description: This compact proof helper formats each assertion result for console output and mutates the file-level pass/fail counts. It is used for all proof checks before the script prints its final summary and exits with success or failure.
